sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
GME closes 2025-02-01 with $3.82B of revenue (-27.5% YoY) and $131M of net income (1859.7% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of -0.7% and a net margin of 3.4%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Earnings held up better than revenue, suggesting that expense control or mix changes partially offset the softer top line.

Operating cash flow reached $146M versus -$204M in the prior period, and after $16M of capital spending the business produced $130M of free cash flow versus -$239M in the prior period. Capital spending absorbed 0.4% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Liquidity and leverage are both relatively manageable, with a current ratio of 8.05x and debt-to-equity of 0.01x. The quick ratio of 7.33x sits below the current ratio, indicating that inventory or other less-liquid current assets carry part of the coverage burden.

paragraphs.text: GME closes 2025-02-01 with $3.82B of revenue (-27.5% YoY) and $131M of net income (1859.7% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of -0.7% and a net margin of 3.4%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Earnings held up better than revenue, suggesting that expense control or mix changes partially offset the softer top line.

Operating cash flow reached $146M versus -$204M in the prior period, and after $16M of capital spending the business produced $130M of free cash flow versus -$239M in the prior period. Capital spending absorbed 0.4% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Liquidity and leverage are both relatively manageable, with a current ratio of 8.05x and debt-to-equity of 0.01x. The quick ratio of 7.33x sits below the current ratio, indicating that inventory or other less-liquid current assets carry part of the coverage burden.

sections.title: Trend Analysis
sections.rendered_content:
Across the annual periods shown here, operating cash flow provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $146M and a compound annual growth rate of -6.9%. The decline is gradual, but the direction remains negative across the observed periods. The main trend relationship is one of weaker scale but better operating discipline.

Cash generation remained constructive, with $146M of operating cash flow translating into $130M of free cash flow. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (0.01x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (8.05x current ratio), because those measures do not always point to the same conclusion about financial flexibility.

sections.title: Risk Factors
sections.rendered_content: The most important risks in the current numbers are concentrated in the areas where profitability, cash generation, or balance-sheet support are not moving together. Revenue declined 27.5% versus prior year.

paragraphs.text: The most important risks in the current numbers are concentrated in the areas where profitability, cash generation, or balance-sheet support are not moving together. Revenue declined 27.5% versus prior year.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
The most constructive part of GME's profile is straightforward: Current ratio of 8.05x provides a wide cushion against near-term obligations. That matters because leverage (0.01x debt-to-equity), liquidity (8.05x current ratio), and free cash flow ($130M) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-02-01, with the prior period set at 2024-02-03. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.
